element_description: Page body element - the root container of all page content
computed_style_change: position: absolute -> static; overflow: hidden -> visible
difference: The original page body uses position:absolute with overflow:hidden (likely for modal overlay management), while the migrated version uses static positioning with visible overflow. This causes fundamentally different page layout behavior.

element_description: Main content container with centered layout and padding
difference: The original page constrains content sections to a max-width of ~1164px centered with auto margins (378px on each side) and inner padding of 15px/12px. The migrated version has no width constraint, causing content to stretch full-width across 1920px with no centering or padding.

element_description: Product carousel container that displays items in a horizontal scrollable row
computed_style_change: display: flex -> block; overflow-x: scroll -> visible
difference: The original uses flexbox display with horizontal scroll for product carousels (showing multiple products in a row). The migrated version uses block display with no scroll, causing product items to stack vertically instead of displaying in a horizontal carousel layout.

element_description: Footer section containing customer service links, social media, and legal info
difference: The footer section font size is reduced from 16px to 12px (a 4px/25% reduction) with corresponding line-height reduction from 24px to 18px. Background changes from white to a light gray (rgb(248,249,249)). Footer text will appear noticeably smaller and the background slightly different.

element_description: Footer sub-section with brand links and legal text
difference: The original footer section has a distinct blue-gray background (rgb(220,225,227)) with 25px vertical padding, giving it a visually separated appearance. The migrated version uses a lighter gray background (rgb(248,249,249)) with no padding, making it blend more with surrounding content.

element_description: Product display section using table layout for multi-column content
difference: The original uses CSS table display for a structured multi-column product layout at 1164px width. The migrated version uses block display at full viewport width, losing the columnar table structure.

computed_style_change: max-width: 100% -> none; overflow: clip -> visible; font-size: 14px -> 16px
difference: The original constrains images with max-width:100% and uses overflow:clip to prevent images from exceeding container bounds. The migrated version removes the max-width constraint and sets overflow to visible, which could cause images to overflow their containers.

computed_style_change: display: flex -> block; overflow-x: scroll -> visible; width: 1072px -> 1920px
difference: The original displays products in a horizontally scrollable flex container at 1072px width. The migrated version changes to block layout at full width with no scroll, fundamentally changing the product browsing interaction pattern.

element_description: Grid row container using negative margins for gutters
difference: The original uses a Bootstrap-style grid system with negative margins (-12px) to create gutters between columns at 1164px container width. The migrated version removes this grid system, losing the columnar gutter spacing.

element_description: Content column with inner padding for spacing
difference: Inner content padding of 12px on each side is removed in the migrated version, causing content to touch the edges of its container without any breathing room.
